mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02) across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day SPX returns (win rate < 45%) in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and corporate revenue growth, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during economic expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day SPX returns during expansion periods (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP reflects underlying economic strength and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction (win rate = 50%) across all historical samples.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds a 3.0% annualized growth rate, anticipated monetary tightening and margin compression force negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 3.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal yields a negative 20-day SPX win rate below 40% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during recessionary periods signals collapsing corporate output and earnings, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during recessions fail to predict negative 5-day SPX returns (positive return frequency > 55%).
